summary: Iteration 9 verification — Admin Dashboard donut chart, Quick Answer reorder, full dark-mode sweep. 324/324 pytest assertions pass. Frontend dark-mode visual checks confirm all called-out elements render with dark backgrounds and light readable text (topic-hub callout, popular-searches badges, FAQ accordion, deep-cluster, dashboard donut). Quick Answer is correctly positioned below the product grid. Donut chart renders with Chart.js + legend + total revenue (conditionally loaded only when paid orders exist).

issues: Hub-link badge ([data-testid=category-hub-link]) uses brand red rgb(220,38,38) on dark rgba(30,41,59,0.85) bg — readable but slightly low contrast vs the WCAG AA target. Optional polish: switch to a lighter brand hue (e.g. #ef4444 or pure white) on the dark variant for better punch.

action_items: (Optional polish) Consider raising contrast of [data-testid=category-hub-link] text in dark mode — current brand red on dark bg works but is the lowest-contrast element seen.
critical_code_review_comments: Sales-by-Category SQL correctly uses oi.qty * oi.price (verified via /admin.php?tab=dashboard returning 200 with no order_items column warnings in body).
Chart.js CDN is gated on $catTotalRev > 0 — confirmed by test that chart.umd.min.js presence === has_data flag.
CSS cache-busting works: ?v=<filemtime> appended to dark-mode-polish.css link in every page tested.
seed_data_creation: None — used existing seed data (1 paid order = $229 in office-2024-pc category).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Playwright UI login via form.fill is flaky on this preview (the login button click does not persist the session — fields show as empty after Sign In). Workaround: use requests.Session() to authenticate, extract PHPSESSID, then inject via page.context.add_cookies(). All backend tests use requests.Session() which works fine. Dark-mode toggle requires BOTH localStorage.setItem('uc_theme','dark') AND document.documentElement.setAttribute('data-bs-theme','dark') because the inline header script reads localStorage on hydration but the data attribute is what CSS uses.
rca_of_the_issue: N/A — no defects found in this iteration.
